title: Vitis bakeri
taxonomy: Vitis bakeri is a species of grapevine that belongs to the genus Vitis. It is classified under the family Vitaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the grape or wine family. Vitis bakeri is native to China and is known to be an evergreen climber. The classification of Vitis bakeri is further divided into the order Rosales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes various types of trees and shrubs. This species is closely related to other Vitis species, such as Vitis vinifera, which is the primary species used for wine production.

properties: Vitis bakeri, also known as the Baker's grape or Chinese grape, is a species of grapevine native to China. intro: Vitis bakeri, also known as Baker's grapevine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative disorders. The bioactive constituents of Vitis bakeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
